analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$76,000 as of Apr 30 08:00 UTC (range $75,652-$76,283 across sources). Down from weekly high ~$79K. Twice rejected at $80K resistance. Support ~$75,000. Volume at lowest since Oct 2023 — fragile, low-depth environment.
sentiment_key_signals: FOMC COMPLETED Apr 29: held 3.5-3.75%; unusual 8-4 dissent — most divided vote since Oct 1992
Still within 48h post-FOMC window — historical BTC weakness pattern (8 of last 9 meetings) still active
BTC twice rejected at $80K resistance — technical ceiling firm
Spot BTC ETF outflows: $263.2M (Apr 27) + $89.7M (Apr 28) — two confirmed consecutive outflow days
ETF outflows yesterday $263M vs $2B total April inflows — short-term sentiment reversing
BTC trading volume below $8B — lowest since Oct 2023, high sensitivity to macro shocks
Brent crude >$104/barrel (Hormuz restrictions) — sustained inflation headwind
GDP Q1 advance + PCE due Apr 30 12:30 UTC — Atlanta Fed nowcast ~1.2% vs consensus 2.5-3%
KelpDAO $290M exploit weighing on DeFi/altcoin sentiment
Geopolitical tensions (oil, global) weighing on risk assets
Fear & Greed Index recovering from lows but not yet bullish
analysis.macro_backdrop: Post-FOMC: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% with 8-4 dissent (most divided since Oct 1992). Powell's final Chair meeting adds transition uncertainty. Oil >$104 (Hormuz) keeps inflation elevated. GDP Q1 advance + PCE releasing today Apr 30 12:30 UTC — stagflation risk high if GDP misses and PCE beats. Two consecutive days of ETF outflows and $80K double top reinforce bearish short-term bias.
analysis.regime_rationale: Bearish (0.60): FOMC bearish 48h pattern still active, $80K double rejection, consecutive ETF outflows, declining volume, Hormuz oil headwinds, weak GDP nowcast, geopolitical tensions. Confidence 0.60 (>=0.5 threshold) — macro filter ACTIVE. Prefer SHORT bias or cautious HOLD. Reassess after GDP/PCE release at 12:30 UTC.
